title: sapium glandulosum
intro: Sapium glandulosum, also known as the stinking cedar, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and the Indian subcontinent. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sapium glandulosum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant is traditionally prepared as a herbal decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in modern medicine to create various herbal preparations and supplements.
